meaning: adj.未公开的,未公布的 未经宣传的
examples: No matter how insignificant or unpublicized something publicly available may be, either though an automated search or though some manual labor, anyone interested in finding that data will get it. 18.
There's an unpublicized view among foreign-car lobbyists that General Motors is much scarier dead or bankrupt than alive.
Eisner dropped in for an unpublicized chat with Senate Majority Leader Trent Lott.
